chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: here we have talked about slicing in numpy arrays

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: is similar to python list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: but there is a big difference in terms of how internally slicing takes place

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: basically whenever we are performing slice operation

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so in python list slice operation creates a copy of our original array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: whereas in numpy arrays

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: instead of copy a view is created

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: copy means that if let's suppose this is our memory

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: this is the computer memory

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: in computer memory we had a list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: in the list we had some values

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: and we took some part of the array by slicing it

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so what will happen is a copy will be created in the memory for this slice

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: but in the case of numpy array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: a new copy will not be created

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: a view will be created

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: view means we are looking at the original values

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: no new copy has been created

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: the answer we are getting is actually a sub part of the array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: it is the sub array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: but these are the original values in the memory

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: no extra memory has been used

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: and because no extra memory is used

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: that's why numpy arrays are fast

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: python list creates an extra copy

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so it will take time

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: any operation we will perform

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: in which extra memory will be required

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: some data will have to be stored again in the memory

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: it will definitely take time

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: that is why python lists are slow

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: numpy array will not store any copy of this type

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: will not take any extra memory

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: it will simply show us the part

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: that is why numpy arrays are fast

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: and we can verify this

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: for example if we want to verify

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: the copy in list vs view in numpy array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: we can take an example of a simple list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: let's suppose we have a simple list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: this is a simple number list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: which we have created in python

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: we will create a sub list from this

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: sub list means our slice list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so in numbers

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: let's suppose we want to go from first index to third index

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so we will get our sub list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: we can print the sub list if we want

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so we have our 2 and 3 sub list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: now in this sub list what do i want to do

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: in this sub list at index 0

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: what will be the index value of this sub list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: this index value 2

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: i want to replace this 2 with 200

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so we have replaced 2 with 200

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: as soon as we have done this

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: we will print our sub list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: and along with that we will print our original list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so we have printed 2 with 200

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so it has changed in sub list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: but original list is still the same

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: now in this same thing we are going to do

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: in this same thing we are going to do with our numpy array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so let's comment this out

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: in numpy array we have created our numpy array from a list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: now we are creating a sub array from this

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: we can print the sub array if we want

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: let's cut this

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so what happened to sub array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: same sub array which we had got printed

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: now in this sub array at index 0

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: i am going to change 2 to 200

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: after that again we will print the sub array and our array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: here we have created our sub array from our original list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so let's cut it out

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: now we want to create it from our array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so we have created it from array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: we have got 2,3

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: after that we will replace 2 with 200 in sub array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: after that again we will print sub array and array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so it has changed in sub array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: but along with that we will notice that it has changed in our array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: why it has changed

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: because when we are replacing this 2 value

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so in the case of python list

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: because copy is being created

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so here we are replacing 2 with 200

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: but in case of numpy arrays

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: no copy is being created

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: and we are changing 2 to 200 in original

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: that is why sub array is also coming different

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: and original array is also coming different

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so this is the difference

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: whenever slice operation is performed in arrays

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: copy is not created

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: a view is created

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: means we are seeing our sub array in a new way

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: but let's suppose we have a use case

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: in which we actually want to create a copy

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so how we will create it

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: for that we can use the function called dot copy

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: we can use the dot copy function

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: by which our copy will be created

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: and after that the original changes

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: will not reflect in the original array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: means changes of sub array and slice

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: will not reflect in our original array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: but here also we have to pay attention

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: that we are not actually copying the entire array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: we are copying the view of the sliced array

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: into this variable

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: so this is an important difference

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: which we have to pay attention to

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: and these are the major operations

chunks.title: Copy
chunks.text: which we perform on numpy arrays

text: here we have talked about slicing in numpy arrays is similar to python list but there is a big difference in terms of how internally slicing takes place basically whenever we are performing slice operation so in python list slice operation creates a copy of our original array whereas in numpy arrays instead of copy a view is created copy means that if let's suppose this is our memory this is the computer memory in computer memory we had a list in the list we had some values and we took some part of the array by slicing it so what will happen is a copy will be created in the memory for this slice but in the case of numpy array a new copy will not be created a view will be created view means we are looking at the original values no new copy has been created the answer we are getting is actually a sub part of the array it is the sub array but these are the original values in the memory no extra memory has been used and because no extra memory is used that's why numpy arrays are fast python list creates an extra copy so it will take time any operation we will perform in which extra memory will be required some data will have to be stored again in the memory it will definitely take time that is why python lists are slow numpy array will not store any copy of this type will not take any extra memory it will simply show us the part that is why numpy arrays are fast and we can verify this for example if we want to verify the copy in list vs view in numpy array we can take an example of a simple list let's suppose we have a simple list this is a simple number list which we have created in python we will create a sub list from this sub list means our slice list so in numbers let's suppose we want to go from first index to third index so we will get our sub list we can print the sub list if we want so we have our 2 and 3 sub list now in this sub list what do i want to do in this sub list at index 0 what will be the index value of this sub list this index value 2 i want to replace this 2 with 200 so we have replaced 2 with 200 as soon as we have done this we will print our sub list and along with that we will print our original list so we have printed 2 with 200 so it has changed in sub list but original list is still the same now in this same thing we are going to do in this same thing we are going to do with our numpy array so let's comment this out in numpy array we have created our numpy array from a list now we are creating a sub array from this we can print the sub array if we want let's cut this so what happened to sub array same sub array which we had got printed now in this sub array at index 0 i am going to change 2 to 200 after that again we will print the sub array and our array here we have created our sub array from our original list so let's cut it out now we want to create it from our array so we have created it from array we have got 2,3 after that we will replace 2 with 200 in sub array after that again we will print sub array and array so it has changed in sub array but along with that we will notice that it has changed in our array why it has changed because when we are replacing this 2 value so in the case of python list because copy is being created so here we are replacing 2 with 200 but in case of numpy arrays no copy is being created and we are changing 2 to 200 in original that is why sub array is also coming different and original array is also coming different so this is the difference whenever slice operation is performed in arrays copy is not created a view is created means we are seeing our sub array in a new way but let's suppose we have a use case in which we actually want to create a copy so how we will create it for that we can use the function called dot copy we can use the dot copy function by which our copy will be created and after that the original changes will not reflect in the original array means changes of sub array and slice will not reflect in our original array but here also we have to pay attention that we are not actually copying the entire array we are copying the view of the sliced array into this variable so this is an important difference which we have to pay attention to and these are the major operations which we perform on numpy arrays
